Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-11-08T09:57:47Z","updated_at":"2024-11-08T13:59:17Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

In numpy 2.1 or later, multiplying a vector-row and a square matrix causes the kernel dying, if the vector dimension is greater 300. I use Spyder (IPython) on Windows 10.
This bug is not present in version 1.26.4.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np # version 2.1
import numpy.random as rn
n = 300
m = 1
x = rn.rand(n, m)
B = rn.rand(n, n)

y = B @ A # No errors
z = A.T @ B # IPython wrote: The kernel died, restarting...
# if m == 2, there are no errors
```


### Error message:

```shell
The kernel died, restarting...
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

Python 3.12.3 | packaged by conda-forge
Numpy 2.1 or later

### Runtime Environment:

_No response_

During installing numpy 1.26.4, some modules' versions were downgraded, I don't remember which ones exactly. Now I can't reinstall numpy 2.1 to find out the modules' versions and experiment with numpy outside Spyder, because I have work to do. After some time, I will reinstall   numpy 2.1 and write to you.

Best regards, Victor.


>Пятница,  8 ноября 2024, 16:38 +03:00 от Sebastian Berg ***@***.***>:
>
>Thanks, presumably an issue with the conda-forge version of blas, but we don't even know which one you are using.Two asks.  First, please confirm that this happens outside of Spyder (with a plain python session).  It's a long time ago, but there have been bad library preloads related to this before (I seem to remember Spyder specifically, but I am not sure).
>Next thing: Please share conda list to show that, you can try picking another one explicitly (e.g. openblas or mkl, I don't know).  You could also pip install numpy, although it's not ideal with conda.
>It may also be good to see what conda list was for the NumPy 1.26.x setup, since the NumPy version is very unlikely to have anything to do with it.
>--
>In general, this is almost certainly going to be an issue on the conda-forge packaging side (or maybe having to update whichever blas version is packaged or so).
>It would be very surprising if NumPy itself has anything to do with the issue.
>—
